summary_en: 18 yen/share for FY3/26 (up 3 yen from prior forecast of 15 yen); prior year 14 yen. New policy: progressive dividend (no cuts absent major events) with DOE target 4.0%. FY3/27 forecast 19 yen/share (8 yen interim + 11 yen year-end). Effective from FY3/26.
title_en: Notice of Change in Dividend Policy (Introduction of Progressive Dividend and DOE Target) and Dividend Increase
translation_en_brief: On 14 May 2026, Medix Inc.'s board of directors approved a revision to its dividend policy and an increase in the FY3/26 year-end dividend. The company is introducing a "progressive dividend" framework coupled with a target dividend-on-equity (DOE) of approximately 4.0%. Under the new policy, the company commits to maintaining or increasing dividends absent material adverse events, moving away from its prior payout-ratio target of 10–20%. The FY3/26 year-end dividend is increased to 18 yen per share, up 3 yen from the prior forecast of 15 yen and compared to 14 yen in FY3/25. Total dividend outlay for FY3/26 is 138 million yen, effective 11 June 2026. Beginning FY3/27, the company will introduce an interim dividend; the FY3/27 full-year dividend is forecast at 19 yen per share (8 yen interim, 11 yen year-end). The policy change reflects the company's established financial strength in its digital-marketing-support business and prioritizes long-term, stable shareholder returns with capital-efficiency discipline.
